pr.title: Fix GObjEvent function signatures in fighter.c
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Updated fighter GObj event callback signatures to take only HSD_GObj* instead of extra dummy or unused parameters, allowing callback registration in Fighter_Create without void* casts. Added an inline HSD_GObjGetUserData helper in gobj.h and used it when extracting Fighter* from a GObj. Small matching-oriented rewrites in Fighter_8006C27C introduced inline position getters, post-decrement syntax, explicit "fighter.c" in __assert, and temporaries for OSReport coordinates.
what_changed: Changed Fighter_procUpdate from void Fighter_procUpdate(HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj, s32 dummy) to void Fighter_procUpdate(HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj) in both fighter.c and fighter.h.
Changed Fighter_8006C27C from a four-argument signature with unused parameters to void Fighter_8006C27C(HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj) in both fighter.c and fighter.h.
Removed void* casts from func_8038FD54 callback registration for Fighter_procUpdate and Fighter_8006C27C inside Fighter_Create.
Added static inline void* HSD_GObjGetUserData(HSD_GObj* gobj) in include/sysdolphin/baselib/gobj.h, returning gobj->user_data.
Replaced direct fighter_gobj->user_data reads in the touched functions with casts from HSD_GObjGetUserData(fighter_gobj).
Added static inline float Fighter_GetPosX(Fighter* fp) and Fighter_GetPosY(Fighter* fp) helpers near Fighter_8006C27C.

Adjusted matching-sensitive local code in Fighter_8006C27C: changed x19C subtraction assignment to x19C--, stored position coordinates in local f32 x and y before OSReport, and replaced __assert(__FILE__, 2590, "0") with __assert("fighter.c", 2590, "0").
smart_moves: Removing the dummy callback parameters made the function declarations match how these GObj event callbacks are registered and eliminated unsafe-looking void* casts at the registration site.
Centralizing GObj user data access behind HSD_GObjGetUserData follows the baselib abstraction pattern and reduces repeated direct field access.
The inline getter and local-temporary rewrites are small, low-risk decomp tactics that can influence codegen without changing behavior.
Keeping the signature fixes synchronized between fighter.c definitions and fighter.h declarations avoided prototype drift.
decomp_lessons: When a callback must be cast to void* at registration, check whether the local function prototype has accumulated incorrect dummy parameters rather than preserving the cast.
For GObj event callbacks in this fighter context, evidence from this PR supports single-argument HSD_GObj* signatures for Fighter_procUpdate and Fighter_8006C27C.
Inline accessors can be introduced in headers or local C files to express known SDK-style operations while preserving matching-sensitive code generation.
String-literal file names in __assert may be required for matching; replacing __FILE__ with the expected literal can be intentional in decomp code.
Very small expression-shape changes, such as using post-decrement or staging OSReport arguments in locals, can be part of a matching fix and should not be dismissed as cosmetic.
naming_conventions: Reviewer requested capitalization as HSD_GObjGetUserData, not HSD_GobjGetUserData; preserve the capitalized GObj component in baselib helper names.
The new helper follows HSD_* baselib naming and returns void* for generic user data.
Local fighter position helpers were named Fighter_GetPosX and Fighter_GetPosY, using the Fighter_ prefix and descriptive accessor names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed callback casts after fixing signatures, which likely improved type correctness while maintaining or improving matchability.
Used static inline helpers for HSD_GObjGetUserData and fighter position access to affect emitted code without adding callable symbols.
Introduced f32 x and f32 y locals before OSReport instead of passing struct fields directly.
Used __assert("fighter.c", 2590, "0") instead of __assert(__FILE__, 2590, "0") in the touched assertion.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: Suggested renaming the new inline helper to static inline void* HSD_GObjGetUserData(HSD_GObj* gobj), correcting capitalization from HSD_GobjGetUserData.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.feedback: Replied "Done", and the diff shows the final helper name as HSD_GObjGetUserData.

follow_up_queries: Find other func_8038FD54 registrations that still require void* casts and verify whether their callback signatures are wrong.
Search for direct HSD_GObj user_data access that could use HSD_GObjGetUserData where matching permits.
Audit GObj event callback prototypes in fighter.h for extra dummy or unused parameters.
Compare capitalization of HSD_GObj helper names across baselib headers to keep naming consistent.
